top_weaknesses.issue: Anchor-text claim '61% of global VC investment in 2025' links to a URL slug that says 'ai-captures-50-percent' — credibility mismatch on-page
top_weaknesses.fix: Reconcile the number (verify source) and update either anchor or URL/citation to match

top_weaknesses.issue: Stays clean and abstract — strong claims, not enough vivid concrete proof (no named agency, no founder quote, no real numbers, no failure mode)
top_weaknesses.fix: Add one vivid agency vignette: a name, a workflow, numbers, a quote, or a documented failure mode

top_weaknesses.issue: Long 'Charge per qualified meeting…per onboarded customer' grocery-belt list inside a single sentence
top_weaknesses.fix: Break into a scannable bulleted list or split after 4 items

top_weaknesses.issue: Final H2 'Agencies Become Products' is softer than the others; first paragraph under it ('It is not fewer agencies. It is fewer agencies that look like the old kind.') trips its own logic
top_weaknesses.fix: Sharpen the H2 (e.g., 'The Winning Agency Looks Like Software') and rewrite that opening line

top_weaknesses.issue: Mild VC-memo texture: 'human trust at the boundary, machine execution in the middle, ruthless measurement at the end' is three-part-symmetry; 'more economically violent' is try-hard
top_weaknesses.fix: Trim three-part-symmetry phrasing; replace 'economically violent' with concrete language

consensus_summary: Best-performing draft so far: clean H2 spine, sharp opener, real thesis, and a quotable closing line ('the billable hour was a tax on coordination'). Mostly held back by an unverified 61%/50% citation mismatch and not enough concrete proof inside an otherwise tight argument.
